title: Paneer Butter Masala
tagline: Creamy, buttery tomato-cashew makhani with soft paneer cubes.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients. This is a rich gravy — most of the calories are butter, cream and cashews.

Lean it out: drop the cream to 2 tbsp and finish with 3 tbsp of hung curd / Greek yogurt whisked in off the heat instead. Saves ~120 kcal a serving and bumps protein. Use half the butter (1 tbsp) and you save another ~100 kcal.

Protein boost: this is already paneer-heavy (~36 g protein from 200 g paneer), but you can push it further by adding 100 g of soaked, squeezed soya chunks to the gravy at the simmer stage (+~30 g protein for the pair), or just use 250 g paneer.
Low-fat paneer swaps in cleanly here and cuts ~150 kcal per serving with no real taste hit in a gravy this rich.

items: 12-15 cashews (~30 g), soaked in warm water 10 min
1 tsp Kashmiri red chilli powder (for colour)
200 g paneer, cut into 2 cm cubes

steps.title: Cook the tomato base
steps.body: Heat 1 tbsp oil + 1 tbsp butter in a pan on medium. Add the chopped onion (~120 g) and cook 4-5 min until soft, then add 3 chopped tomatoes (~350 g) and the soaked 30 g cashews. Cook 8-10 minutes until the tomatoes fully break down and the oil starts to separate. Tip: if you added tomatoes in two batches and they're uneven, splash in 2-3 tbsp water, cover, drop to medium-low and stir every 2 min for 5-7 min so they all soften together before you blend.

steps.title: Blend smooth
steps.body: Cool slightly, then blend the tomato-cashew-onion mix into a completely smooth puree. Pass it through a strainer if you want it silky like the restaurant version.

steps.title: Bloom the spices
steps.body: In the same pan, melt 1 tbsp (15 g) butter. Add 1.5 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and saute 30-45 seconds until the raw smell goes — do NOT skip this. Add 1 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder and 1/2 tsp turmeric, stir for 10 seconds so they don't burn.

steps.title: Simmer the gravy
steps.body: Pour the blended puree back in. Add 1 tsp sugar and 3/4 tsp salt. Simmer on low 8-10 minutes, stirring, until it thickens and darkens and butter beads on the surface.

steps.title: Add paneer and cream
steps.body: Stir in 3 tbsp (45 ml) cream and 1/2 tsp garam masala, then slide in the 200 g paneer cubes. Simmer gently 3-4 minutes — don't boil hard or the cream can split.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Turn off the heat. Crush 1 tsp kasuri methi between your palms and stir it in with a final 1 tbsp (15 g) butter. Garnish with 2 tbsp chopped coriander. Rest 5 minutes before serving — the flavour rounds out.

notes: Forgot the ginger-garlic paste? Don't dump it in raw at the end. Heat a little butter in a small pan, saute 1-1.5 tsp of the paste for 30-45 seconds until the raw smell goes, then stir that into the finished gravy and simmer 2-3 min. Raw paste added late tastes harsh.
For extra silkiness soak the paneer in warm water for 10 min before adding — it stays soft.
No cream on hand? A homemade Greek-yogurt-style hung curd (strain regular dahi in a cloth for 3-4 hours) works and adds protein — just whisk it in off the heat so it doesn't curdle.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
